name: test-coverage
description: Maximize test coverage: add missing tests, cover edge cases, improve assertion quality

eval-coverage.justification: Highest weight: coverage is the primary optimization target. Line and branch coverage correlate with defect detection probability. Higher phaseTransitionPct (0.5) because coverage improvements are larger-grained than quality improvements.
eval-coverage.reference: ISO/IEC 25010:2011 Section 4.2.2; Mockus et al. 'Test Coverage and Post-Verification Defects' (ESEM 2009)

eval-tests.justification: Pass rate ensures new tests actually pass. High coverage with failing tests is worse than low coverage with passing tests.

eval-lint.justification: Minimal weight: lint guards against sloppy test code but is not the optimization target. Test files should still follow coding standards.

eval-llm.justification: LLM evaluates test quality dimensions coverage tools miss: assertion specificity, edge case selection, test readability, and maintainability of test code itself.
eval-llm.reference: ISO/IEC 25010:2011 Section 4.2.6; Athanasiou et al. 'Test Code Quality and Its Relation to Issue Handling Performance' (IEEE TSE 2014)
